Course Title: AI in Network Optimisation: Risk vs Reliability
Category: Technology & Telecommunications: Information Technology, Science & Technology, Software, Hardware, Telecom
Who is this for: Network administrators, IT managers, telecommunications engineers, systems administrators, infrastructure engineers, network operations center staff, IT directors, technology managers
Team or Dept this is for: IT, Network Operations, Telecommunications, Infrastructure, Operations, Engineering
Description: When AI-driven routing and optimisation go wrong.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with computer networks and an interest in how artificial intelligence is applied to telecommunications are all you need to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with AI-driven routing and network optimisation, and explain how failures in these systems can impact reliability across telecommunications infrastructure. You will also apply practical strategies to evaluate, monitor, and mitigate the effects of AI optimisation errors, helping you make more informed decisions when managing or designing resilient network environments.
